title: G6: Part (b) — functional equation of the L-genus and its geometric interpretation

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Under-specified in the problem; must be pinned down.** Part (b) asks for a geometric interpretation of the functional equation of the virtual signature / $L$-genus, 'analogous to that for $x/\tanh x$'. A rigorous answer must:

1. **Define** the virtual signature / $L$-genus of a (virtual) oriented bundle via the multiplicative sequence attached to $f(z)=z\coth z$.
2. **State the functional equation precisely.** $f$ is even, $f(z)=f(-z)$; $\coth$ satisfies the addition law $\coth(u+v)=\frac{1+\coth u\coth v}{\coth u+\coth v}$ and periodicity $\coth(z+i\pi)=\coth z$, equivalently the partial-fraction expansion $z\coth z=1+\sum_{n\ge1}\frac{2z^2}{z^2+n^2\pi^2}$.
3. **Prove the geometric interpretation.** Most defensibly: evenness $\leftrightarrow$ Poincaré-duality symmetry of the intersection form; the multiplicative/addition law of $\coth$ $\leftrightarrow$ **multiplicativity of the signature in fibre bundles** (Chern–Hirzebruch–Serre: for a fibration $F\to E\to B$ with $\pi_1(B)$ acting trivially on $H^*(F;\mathbb{R})$, $\tau(E)=\tau(B)\tau(F)$). The solver must give a genuine theorem-level statement and proof, not a heuristic analogy. Specify exactly which functional equation is intended and justify the geometric correspondence.
